response.global_summary: The video features a virtual stage performance by VTubers Usada Pekora and AZKi singing 'RE:I AM' by Aimer. The performance is continuous throughout the video, featuring both characters alternating verses and singing together.

scenes.description: Usada Pekora and AZKi perform 'RE:I AM' on a virtual stage. The stage backdrop changes colors and patterns throughout the song. The two performers take turns singing individual verses and join together for the chorus, dancing on stage.
scenes.reference_lyrics_en:
Please hear me
I want to tell you
Please sing to me
I wanna hear your voice
While the heartbeats of time still echo
I hid those naked words deep in my chest
The colors of my memory have begun to fade
In the corner of a broken world
I don't want anything but to hold you
Right now that's all I can do
Trembling beneath feigned courage that only looks like pride
Like a lost child
Even my last words will be lost in the roaming winds
As I turned my hands to the moon you vanished into the stars
Even if I hold you and say "Stay by my side"
I won't hear your singing voice again
The siren of a pouring rain
Instead of me the cosmos keeps on crying
Even if I gather up the teachings I've trampled on until now
And press them against my chest
It looks like a game where I'm using my song to ask for salvation
Like adults telling legends
I don't need a love of the skies and guidance
That just clings to words
In this decorated prayer
I can't feel the next step to tomorrow
One day it will surely reach you
This unadorned, nameless poem of my youth
I want to forget it just once
The pain of sleepless nights is holding that poem
Freezing cold shatters my sorrow
And scorching sand puts it together again
Freezing cold shatters my sorrow
And scorching sand puts it together again
If it's a kind of correctness that can be thrown away
Then it's better to have mistakes that will never disappear
If I expose the shyness I've hidden in my voice
Once more with my own hands
Even the trajectory I grasped and the light that bloomed
Looked monochrome due to my dried-out heart
I won't forget the scenery of today
This ordinary wish will light the path before my feet.,reference_lyrics_og:

response.thoughts:
**Analyzing the VTuber Performance**

Okay, so I've got this video to analyze – it seems to be a performance by two VTubers, likely Usada Pekora and Azki, performing "RE: I AM" by Aimer. The task is to give a detailed breakdown, basically a transcription and some scene analysis.

First things first, I need to figure out how to structure this. Given that it's a continuous musical performance, I think the most efficient approach is to treat it as one single scene, unless the visuals or performance style shifts drastically within the song. The prompt specifies "chronological scenes mapping the entire segment". Since it's a continuous performance of the song, it seems best to just have one scene.

Now, the core of this task will be identifying the song and then retrieving the lyrics. The on-screen text "RE: I AM" confirms the song title, and the context points towards Aimer as the artist. I'll need to do some web searching for the lyrics in Japanese and, ideally, an English translation as well, just in case.

So, for the search, I'll use `web_search_langsearch_multi`. I'll start with the following queries: `RE: I AM Aimer 歌詞` for the Japanese lyrics, and `RE: I AM Aimer lyrics English` for a translation.

Once I've got the search results, it's a matter of populating the JSON structure. I'll fill in the `global_summary` with an overview, then define the `scenes` array, placing the retrieved lyrics (or a "null" if I fail to get them) within it. I'll also be sure to include the song's metadata – Title, Artist, Performers, and Language.

And finally, to keep things clean, I need to be absolutely strict about the guidelines: no manual transcription or translations, no using placeholders, and definitely no truncation of the lyrics. Let's get these search queries rolling and see what we can find.



**JSON Construction for "RE:I AM" Performance**

Okay, here's what I've got. I've successfully located both the original Japanese lyrics and an English translation of Aimer's "RE:I AM." I double-checked the translation against another source, Wattpad, and it seems accurate enough for my purposes. I'm satisfied.

The video I'm analyzing features Usada Pekora and AZKi performing. It's a single, continuous performance, so I'll be treating it as one scene for the JSON structure.

The core of this work will be the lyrics; I have them both in their original and translated forms, ready to be integrated into the JSON. I'll need to parse this effectively. It's time to build the JSON framework with the lyrics at the center, ready to go.
